description: Abstract states H1 is 'falsified at layer 8 but not at layer 4' — contradiction since falsification is binary, not layer-dependent. The pre-registered falsification criterion uses <10% across layers 4-10 collectively, not per-layer.
fix: Restructure the H1 abstract sentence to distinguish pre-registered falsification at layer 8 (0.19% < 10% threshold) from exploratory layer-4 finding (49.3% > 20% threshold). Example: 'At layer 8 (pre-registered test layer), only 0.19% have Af > 0.5, below the <10% falsification threshold, falsifying H1 at that layer; however, at layer 4 (exploratory), 49.3% have Af > 0.5, a finding requiring further investigation given the collective hypothesis framing.'

issue: Abstract contains admission about unconducted experiment
description: Abstract states 'the correct experiment (comparing full SAE representations at layers with different absorption profiles) was not conducted' — this is a limitation that belongs in Discussion, not as a qualifier on results in the abstract.
fix: Remove this parenthetical entirely from the abstract. The H4 design flaw is properly addressed in Section 5.3 and 6.2. State the uninformative result clearly: 'Both absorption subsets yielded 0.0 faithfulness, preventing any conclusion about whether absorption level predicts circuit-level causal importance.'

issue: H4 framed as falsified when it was never tested
description: H4 is framed as 'falsified' but the experiment does not test the hypothesis because the causal variable was not isolated. The key comparison (low-absorption vs. high-absorption subsets) is uninterpretable because both yielded 0.0 — not because absorption didn't affect faithfulness, but because the subset selection method selected latents irrelevant to the circuit.
fix: Reframe H4 as genuinely untested, not falsified. Add explicit language in Abstract, Section 5.3, and Section 6: 'H4 remains an unconducted experiment — the pilot results do not support any conclusion about whether absorption level predicts circuit-level causal importance. The correct experiment (full SAE at layer 4 vs. layer 8) was not run.'

issue: H2 abandoned despite existing data (260x more variance at layer 4)
description: H2 (token frequency correlation) is listed as pending due to 'early termination.' But layer 4 from H3 has 49.3% absorption (~12,000 latents) — 260x more than layer 8 (46 latents). The data to run H2 already exists.
fix: Either (a) run H2 on existing layer 4 data (which has sufficient variance for Spearman correlation), or (b) explicitly retire H2 with justification: 'insufficient statistical power even at the highest-absorption layer tested.' Do not leave H2 pending when data to resolve it exists. The pre-registered analysis (bin latents by median token frequency, compute Spearman r) can be applied to layer 4 data directly.

description: H3 hypothesizes L1 sparsity increases absorption but uses L0 as a proxy. This conflates training hyperparameter (lambda) with outcome measure (L0) and with layer-specific effects independent of sparsity.
fix: Add explicit language in Discussion: 'H3 tested L0 as a proxy for L1 sparsity penalty, not L1 directly. The finding is that absorption does not increase monotonically with L0. The inverted-U pattern reflects L0 outcomes and layer-specific semantic density, not a direct L1 lambda sweep. Whether the same pattern holds for actual L1 penalty variations requires controlled training experiments.'

description: H5 compares 2K, 8K, 24K using subsampled 24K SAE, not independently trained dictionaries. This confounds dictionary size effect with superset/subset artifact and the 'upper bound' framing introduces additional bias.
fix: Add explicit acknowledgment in Section 5.4 and 6.4: 'The comparison is between a full dictionary and its subselections, not between independently trained SAEs of different sizes. This limits the conclusiveness of the H5 finding — we observe that larger subselections of the 24K SAE show lower absorption than smaller subselections, but this may not generalize to SAEs trained independently at different dictionary sizes.'

description: Section 5.2 text says 'Figure 2 shows the detailed absorption score distribution for layer 4' but Figure 2 is the inverted-U line plot across all layers. The layer-4 histogram is Figure 4.
fix: Correct the figure reference to Figure 4, or verify figure plan numbering matches actual files.

description: Three issues from prior review remain: 'layer-wise' (Section 5.2, prefer 'per-layer'), 'activations' as singular noun (Section 3.1, prefer 'activation'), 'significant' without 'statistical' qualifier (Section 5.2, prefer 'statistical significance').
fix: Global search-and-replace for these three specific terms across the full manuscript.

issue: Interpretive claim overstates evidence ('shows that' -> 'suggests that')
description: Section 5.3: 'The full-vs-subset comparison shows that dictionary completeness — not absorption level — drives patching fidelity.' Flagged in prior review, remains unfixed. Evidence is consistent with interpretation but does not definitively prove it given H4 design flaw.
fix: Change 'shows that' to 'suggests that' throughout. Evidence is consistent with interpretation but cannot definitively prove it given H4 design flaw.

description: 'This is guaranteed because random decoder columns are isotropic in d-dimensional space; the top-5 co-firers span at most 6 dimensions, explaining a vanishing fraction of the 768-dimensional residual stream variance.' Logic is implied but not stated.
fix: Add: isotropic Gaussian columns in 768-dim space have expected squared cosine similarity of 1/768 with any fixed direction. A 6-dimensional subspace captures expected 6/768 = 0.78% of variance, confirming that random decoder co-firers cannot reconstruct substantial variance.

description: 'Approximately 79% of latents have Af <= 0.5' at layer 8 — from Table 2: 20.9% have Af > 0.5 and 76.8% have Af = 0.0, accounting for 97.7%. The 79% derivation is not stated. 76.8% with Af=0.0 is a separate category from Af <= 0.5 but not > 0.5.
fix: Clarify the derivation: 'approximately 79.1% (100 - 20.9) have neither high absorption (Af > 0.5) nor complete independence (Af = 0.0) — they fall in the middle range. Of these, 76.8% have Af = 0.0 exactly and approximately 2.3% have 0 < Af <= 0.5.'

issue: 8 perfect-score latents (Af=1.0, exactly 100 activations each) not investigated
description: 100 = number of sequences suggests position-embedding artifact, not genuine semantic absorption. Regularity is suspicious.
fix: Add brief analysis: do these latents fire at consistent token positions across sequences? Verify whether these are artifacts or genuine absorption.
